concerns.text: Plan Phase 4 explicitly requires 'Re-train the Physics-Informed Training Proxy... on the same raw dataset' to mimic the PhysisForcing baseline. Tasks T033a (loss function), T033b (baseline trainer), and T033c (artifact management) only implement the *components* to build the baseline. There is no task to actually *execute* the baseline training run, generate the baseline model artifacts, or feed them into the evaluation suite (T031-T039). Without an execution task, the comparative analysis in SC-002 and SC-003 cannot be performed.

Spec FR-008 and SC-006 require validation using 'an independent physics engine (MuJoCo) OR real-world data' to ensure non-circularity. Tasks T037 and T038 implement MuJoCo re-scoring but explicitly use the 'same CV-extracted trajectories and prompt-derived initial conditions' as the PyBullet filter. This fails to provide an independent validation of the video content itself (circularity risk) and completely omits the 'real-world data' option mandated by the spec. The tasks do not cover the requirement to validate against actual physical reality or a distinct ground truth source.

Task T042 includes a '[deferred]' placeholder for the RAM reduction target ('reduce peak RAM by [deferred]'). While planning docs can defer empirical values, a task's completion condition must be testable. The task description fails to define a concrete metric or reference for the reduction target, making it impossible to verify if the task is complete. This should reference a specific metric definition or be removed until the target is defined in the spec/plan.
